2026-04-23T09:42:01.0333528Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T09:42:01.0334072Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T09:42:01.0334491Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T09:42:01.0334853Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T09:42:01.0335159Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T09:42:01.0335391Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T09:42:01.0335545Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-23T09:42:01.0335720Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T09:42:01.0335884Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-23T09:42:01.0336111Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-23T09:42:01.0336307Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T09:42:01.0336587Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-23T09:42:01.0336840Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. 2026-04-23T09:44:22.2484663Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T09:42:58.029743154Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58589,\"title\":\"[azure-sdk-mgmt-pr-review] Docs: SKILL.md scanner rule list is incomplete and contains wrong rule ID\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `azure-sdk-mgmt-pr-review` (Copilot skill)\\n**Service directory:** `.github/skills/azure-sdk-mgmt-pr-review/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58577 by `@live1206`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58577 added two new files to the `azure-sdk-mgmt-pr-review` skill:\\n- `Check-MgmtNamingRules.ps1` — a 770-line PowerShell script implementing automated naming rule checks\\n- `SKILL.md` — documentation describing the skill\\u0026#39;s phases and review checklist\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\nThe \\u0026#34;scanner emits these rule families\\u0026#34; list in `SKILL.md` (Phase 2, step 3, lines 66–72) has the following issues:\\n\\n- **Wrong rule ID:** `DATETIME001` is listed, but the script uses `DATE001` (see `Check-MgmtNamingRules.ps1` line 337: `$ExcludeRules -notcontains \\u0026#39;DATE001\\u0026#39;`). This means reviewers cannot correctly suppress the rule via `-ExcludeRules DATE001` if they follow the SKILL.md.\\n- **Missing `TYPE001`:** The script implements a `TYPE001` rule (\\u0026#34;string-typed properties that should use strong types\\u0026#34;, line 368) but it is not mentioned in the SKILL.md scanner list.\\n- **Missing `ENUM001`:** The script implements `ENUM001` (\\u0026#34;plural enum names should be singular\\u0026#34;, line 608) but it is absent from the SKILL.md scanner list.\\n- **Missing `METHOD001`:** The script implements `METHOD001` (\\u0026#34;ListOperations methods should be removed\\u0026#34;, line 664) but it is absent from the SKILL.md scanner list.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `.github/skills/azure-sdk-mgmt-pr-review/SKILL.md`\\n**Action:** Edit\\n\\nLocate the bullet list under the heading \\u0026#34;The scanner currently emits these rule families\\u0026#34; (currently lines 66–72). Replace the current list with the corrected, complete list:\\n\\n**Current (incorrect):**\\n```markdown\\n   The scanner currently emits these rule families (see \\u0026#34;API Review Checklist\\u0026#34; for details):\\n   - `SUFFIX001`–`SUFFIX010` – forbidden type-name suffixes (Parameters, Request, Options, Response, Data, Definition, Operation, Collection, **Update**, ...)\\n   - `RESINFIX001` – `Resource` infix in `*Data`/`*Collection` model names (with PrivateLinkResource exception)\\n   - `ACRONYM001` – curated acronyms in wrong casing (HTTP/TCP/SSL/TLS/...)\\n   - `ACRONYM002` – generic 3+ letter all-caps run inside a name (NNI, IPV, BFD, ...)\\n   - `ARMCOMMON001` – type duplicates an Azure.ResourceManager/Azure.Core common type (`OperationStatusResult`, `ManagedServiceIdentity*`, `TagsUpdate`, `ErrorResponse`, ...)\\n   - `BOOL001` / `DATETIME001` / `TTL001` – property naming\\n```\\n\\n**Replacement (corrected):**\\n```markdown\\n   The scanner currently emits these rule families (see \\u0026#34;API Review Checklist\\u0026#34; for details):\\n   - `SUFFIX001`–`SUFFIX010` – forbidden type-name suffixes (Parameters, Request, Options, Response, Data, Definition, Operation, Collection, **Update**, ...)\\n   - `RESINFIX001` – `Resource` infix in `*Data`/`*Collection` model names (with PrivateLinkResource exception)\\n   - `ACRONYM001` – curated acronyms in wrong casing (HTTP/TCP/SSL/TLS/...)\\n   - `ACRONYM002` – generic 3+ letter all-caps run inside a name (NNI, IPV, BFD, ...)\\n   - `ARMCOMMON001` – type duplicates an Azure.ResourceManager/Azure.Core common type (`OperationStatusResult`, `ManagedServiceIdentity*`, `TagsUpdate`, `ErrorResponse`, ...)\\n   - `BOOL001` / `DATE001` / `TTL001` – property naming (bool prefix, DateTimeOffset ends with `On`/`At`, TTL renamed to `TimeToLiveIn`)\\n   - `TYPE001` – string-typed properties that should use a strong type (`Guid`, `ResourceIdentifier`, `ResourceType`, `ETag`, `AzureLocation`)\\n   - `ENUM001` – plural enum type names should be singular (unless bit flags)\\n   - `METHOD001` – `ListOperations` methods should be removed\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Validate\\n\\nRun a quick sanity check to confirm the rule IDs in the script match the updated documentation:\\n\\n```bash\\ngrep -n \\u0026#34;ExcludeRules -notcontains\\\\|ExcludeRules -contains\\u0026#34; .github/skills/azure-sdk-mgmt-pr-review/Check-MgmtNamingRules.ps1 | grep -oP \\u0026#34;\\u0026#39;[A-Z]+\\\\d+\\u0026#39;\\u0026#34; | sort -u\\n```\\n\\nConfirm the output matches every rule ID listed in the updated SKILL.md scanner list.\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Replace the current list with the corrected, complete list:\\n\\n**Current (incorrect):**\\n```markdown\\n   The scanner currently emits these rule families (see \\u0026#34;API Review Checklist\\u0026#34; for details):\\n   - `SUFFIX001`–`SUFFIX010` – forbidden type-name suffixes (Parameters, Request, Options, Response, Data, Definition, Operation, Collection, **Update**, ...)\\n   - `RESINFIX001` – `Resource` infix in `*Data`/`*Collection` model names (with PrivateLinkResource exception)\\n   - `ACRONYM001` – curated acronyms in wrong casing (HTTP/TCP/SSL/TLS/...)\\n   - `ACRONYM002` – generic 3+ letter all-caps run inside a name (NNI, IPV, BFD, ...)\\n   - `ARMCOMMON001` – type duplicates an Azure.ResourceManager/Azure.Core common type (`OperationStatusResult`, `ManagedServiceIdentity*`, `TagsUpdate`, `ErrorResponse`, ...)\\n   - `BOOL001` / `DATETIME001` / `TTL001` – property naming\\n```\\n\\n**Replacement (corrected):**\\n```markdown\\n   The scanner currently emits these rule families (see \\u0026#34;API Review Checklist\\u0026#34; for details):\\n   - `SUFFIX001`–`SUFFIX010` – forbidden type-name suffixes (Parameters, Request, Options, Response, Data, Definition, Operation, Collection, **Update**, ...)\\n   - `RESINFIX001` – `Resource` infix in `*Data`/`*Collection` model names (with PrivateLinkResource exception)\\n   - `ACRONYM001` – curated acronyms in wrong casing (HTTP/TCP/SSL/TLS/...)\\n   - `ACRONYM002` – generic 3+ letter all-caps run inside a name (NNI, IPV, BFD, ...)\\n   - `ARMCOMMON001` – type duplicates an Azure.ResourceManager/Azure.Core common type (`OperationStatusResult`, `ManagedServiceIdentity*`, `TagsUpdate`, `ErrorResponse`, ...)\\n   - `BOOL001` / `DATE001` / `TTL001` – property naming (bool prefix, DateTimeOffset ends with `On`/`At`, TTL renamed to `TimeToLiveIn`)\\n   - `TYPE001` – string-typed properties that should use a strong type (`Guid`, `ResourceIdentifier`, `ResourceType`, `ETag`, `AzureLocation`)\\n   - `ENUM001` – plural enum type names should be singular (unless bit flags)\\n   - `METHOD001` – `ListOperations` methods should be removed\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Validate\\n\\nRun a quick sanity check to confirm the rule IDs in the script match the updated documentation:\\n\\n```bash\\ngrep -n \\u0026#34;ExcludeRules -notcontains\\\\|ExcludeRules -contains\\u0026#34; .github/skills/azure-sdk-mgmt-pr-review/Check-MgmtNamingRules.ps1 | grep -oP \\u0026#34;\\u0026#39;[A-Z]+\\\\d+\\u0026#39;\\u0026#34; | sort -u\\n```\\n\\nConfirm the output matches every rule ID listed in the updated SKILL.md scanner list.\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
